patch.value: Strengthen storage, append-only history, and durability primitives (compaction, integrity, gc, export/import)
Strengthen storage, append-only history, and durability primitives (compaction, integrity, gc, export/import)
History chain integrity gates stay green; gc, compact, redact, and repair flows verified on large workspaces; no open data-loss classes.

## Charter
Living capability epic for storage, append-only history, and data durability: TOON item files, per-item history streams (hash-chained JSONL), checkpoints, gc, compaction, export/import, and format_version gating.

## Scope
- In: item-store write paths, history chain integrity (repair/redact/compact), gc scopes (locks/checkpoints/embeddings), backup/restore, format migrations.
- Out: concurrency/locking semantics (pm-dj98 owns races and atomicity), search index storage (pm-ydp6).

## Invariants this epic guards
- History is append-only and hash-chained; appendLineAtomic is the only writer (see pm-y2ml docs).
- Items must survive strict→lenient TOON decode fallback (2026-05-24 recovery precedent).
- pm health history_drift must stay at 0 across 1400+ items.

## Status
20 closed children (compact snapshot, history-compact bulk + policy, gc scopes, restore hardening). Open as a living backlog — no open children at 2026-07-04; new durability work lands here.

## Update 2026-07-06
New initiative epic parented here: **pm-9rxu — Scale-out** (sub-second, bounded-token context at 100k-1M items, thousands of concurrent agents). Owns workspace-size physics: read index, directory fan-out, pagination, claim throughput, history/git growth policy. ADR: pm-bl8x (under pm-m2u1). Boundary: pm-dj98 keeps correctness races; pm-9rxu owns throughput/scale.
